description: Unified gateway for manipulating pages, databases, blocks, comments, and users inside a Notion workspace. Supports full CRUD, search, and pagination while maintaining consistent ISO-8601 timestamps and shared formatting options.

tools.name: notion_append_block_children
tools.description: Append one or more child blocks to an existing parent block.

block_id.description: The unique identifier of the parent block that will receive the children.

children.description: Array of block objects to append under the parent.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

block_id.description: The unique identifier of the parent block that now contains the new children.

children_count.description: Total number of child blocks now present under the parent.

tools.name: notion_retrieve_block_children
tools.description: Fetch the child blocks contained within a given parent block with optional pagination.

block_id.description: The unique identifier of the parent block whose children are requested.

start_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for retrieving the next page of results.

page_size.description: Maximum number of child blocks to return in this page.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

results.description: List of child block objects each containing block_id, type, and content.

next_cursor.description: Cursor for retrieving the next page of children; null when no more pages exist.

tools.name: notion_create_block
tools.description: Create a single new block and attach it to the specified parent page or block.

parent_id.description: The unique identifier of the parent page or block that will contain the new block.

block_type.description: Notion block type such as paragraph, heading_1, bulleted_list_item, etc.

content.description: Structure matching the chosen block_type that defines the block’s content.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

block_id.description: The unique identifier assigned to the newly created block.

type.description: The block type as stored in Notion.

content.description: The content payload persisted for this block.

last_edited_time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent edit.

tools.name: notion_update_block
tools.description: Modify the type, content, or archived status of an existing block.

block_id.description: The unique identifier of the block to update.

block_type.description: New block type if changing the type.

content.description: Updated content structure matching the block type.

archived.description: Whether to archive (true) or restore (false) the block.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

block_id.description: The unique identifier of the updated block.

type.description: The block type after the update.

content.description: The content payload after the update.

last_edited_time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent edit.

tools.name: notion_delete_block
tools.description: Delete (archive) a specific block.

block_id.description: The unique identifier of the block to delete.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

block_id.description: The unique identifier of the deleted block.

deleted.description: True if the block was successfully archived.

tools.name: notion_retrieve_page
tools.description: Fetch metadata and properties of a specific page.

page_id.description: The unique identifier of the page to retrieve.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

page_id.description: The unique identifier of the retrieved page.

properties.description: Key-value property map as defined in the page’s parent database.

last_edited_time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent edit.

tools.name: notion_create_page
tools.description: Create a new page inside a specified parent page or database.

parent.description: Parent descriptor containing either page_id or database_id.

properties.description: Initial property values when the parent is a database.

children.description: Optional blocks to populate the new page body.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

page_id.description: The unique identifier assigned to the newly created page.

parent.description: Parent descriptor as stored in Notion.

properties.description: Properties of the page after creation.

last_edited_time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent edit.

tools.name: notion_delete_page
tools.description: Delete (archive) a specific page.

page_id.description: The unique identifier of the page to delete.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

page_id.description: The unique identifier of the deleted page.

deleted.description: True if the page was successfully archived.

archived.description: Mirrors deleted status; true when the page is in the archive.

tools.name: notion_update_page_properties
tools.description: Update property values of an existing page.

page_id.description: The unique identifier of the page whose properties will be updated.

properties.description: Map of property names to updated values.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

page_id.description: The unique identifier of the updated page.

updated_properties.description: List of property keys that were modified.

last_edited_time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent edit.

tools.name: notion_update_page_content
tools.description: Append or replace content blocks within an existing page.

page_id.description: The unique identifier of the page whose content will be updated.

children.description: Array of block objects to append to the page.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

page_id.description: The unique identifier of the updated page.

children_count.description: Total number of child blocks now present on the page.

last_edited_time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent edit.

tools.name: notion_create_database
tools.description: Create a new database inside a specified parent page or workspace.

parent.description: Parent descriptor containing either page_id or workspace flag.

title.description: Rich-text array representing the database title.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

database_id.description: The unique identifier assigned to the newly created database.

properties.description: Property schema as stored in Notion.

tools.name: notion_query_database
tools.description: Query a database and retrieve pages that match optional filter and sort criteria.

database_id.description: The unique identifier of the database to query.

filter.description: Notion filter object to constrain results.

sorts.description: List of sort objects to order results.

start_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for retrieving the next page of results.

page_size.description: Maximum number of results to return in this page.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

results.description: List of page objects each containing page_id and properties.

next_cursor.description: Cursor for retrieving the next page; null when no more pages exist.

has_more.description: True when additional result pages are available.

tools.name: notion_retrieve_database
tools.description: Fetch metadata and property schema of a specific database.

database_id.description: The unique identifier of the database to retrieve.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

database_id.description: The unique identifier of the retrieved database.

properties.description: Property schema defining columns and types.

last_edited_time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent edit.

tools.name: notion_update_database
tools.description: Modify title, description, or property schema of an existing database.

database_id.description: The unique identifier of the database to update.

title.description: New rich-text title for the database.

description.description: New rich-text description for the database.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

database_id.description: The unique identifier of the updated database.

last_edited_time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent edit.

tools.name: notion_delete_database
tools.description: Delete (archive) a specific database.

database_id.description: The unique identifier of the database to delete.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

database_id.description: The unique identifier of the deleted database.

deleted.description: True if the database was successfully archived.

archived.description: Mirrors deleted status; true when the database is in the archive.

tools.name: notion_create_database_item
tools.description: Add a new row (page) to a database with the provided properties.

database_id.description: The unique identifier of the database that will contain the new item.

properties.description: Property values matching the database schema.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

page_id.description: The unique identifier assigned to the newly created page (database row).

database_id.description: The unique identifier of the database containing this item.

properties.description: Property values as stored after creation.

tools.name: notion_search
tools.description: Global search across pages and databases by title with optional filtering and sorting.

query.description: Text to search for within titles.

filter.description: Restrict results to only pages or only databases.

sort.description: Sort criteria for the returned list.

start_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for retrieving the next page of results.

page_size.description: Maximum number of results to return in this page.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

results.description: List of objects each containing object_id, object_type, and title.

next_cursor.description: Cursor for retrieving the next page; null when no more pages exist.

has_more.description: True when additional result pages are available.

tools.name: notion_list_all_users
tools.description: List every user in the workspace with optional pagination.

start_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for retrieving the next page of users.

page_size.description: Maximum number of users to return in this page.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

results.description: List of user objects each containing user_id, name, and email.

next_cursor.description: Cursor for retrieving the next page; null when no more pages exist.

tools.name: notion_retrieve_user
tools.description: Fetch detailed information about a specific user.

user_id.description: The unique identifier of the user to retrieve.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

user_id.description: The unique identifier of the retrieved user.

email.description: Email address associated with the user account.

type.description: User type such as person or bot.

tools.name: notion_retrieve_bot_user
tools.description: Fetch information about the bot user associated with the current integration token.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

user_id.description: The unique identifier of the bot user.

owner.description: Owner information for this bot integration.

tools.name: notion_create_comment
tools.description: Add a new comment to a page or an existing discussion thread.

rich_text.description: Rich-text array representing the body of the comment.

parent.description: Must contain page_id when starting a new thread.

discussion_id.description: Existing discussion thread ID when replying to a thread.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

comment_id.description: The unique identifier assigned to the newly created comment.

rich_text.description: The rich-text content as stored after creation.

tools.name: notion_retrieve_comments
tools.description: Fetch unresolved comments attached to a page or block with optional pagination.

block_id.description: The unique identifier of the page or block whose comments are requested.

start_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for retrieving the next page of comments.

page_size.description: Maximum number of comments to return in this page.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

results.description: List of comment objects each containing comment_id, rich_text, and created_time.

next_cursor.description: Cursor for retrieving the next page; null when no more pages exist.

tools.name: notion_update_comment
tools.description: Modify the rich-text content of an existing comment.

comment_id.description: The unique identifier of the comment to update.

rich_text.description: New rich-text array replacing the previous content.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

comment_id.description: The unique identifier of the updated comment.

rich_text.description: The rich-text content after the update.

last_edited_time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent edit.

tools.name: notion_delete_comment
tools.description: Delete a specific comment.

comment_id.description: The unique identifier of the comment to delete.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

comment_id.description: The unique identifier of the deleted comment.

deleted.description: True if the comment was successfully removed.

tools.name: notion_resolve_comment
tools.description: Mark a comment as resolved or unresolved.

comment_id.description: The unique identifier of the comment to resolve or unresolve.

resolved.description: True to mark as resolved; false to reopen.

format.description: Response format selector applied across all tools.

comment_id.description: The unique identifier of the resolved comment.

resolved_time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the comment was resolved; absent when unresolved.

resolved_by.description: User who resolved the comment; absent when unresolved.
